behavior_summary: U8P_BUG::BUG::look: When the object is examined (Event 0) it sets instance info/exclude state, emits the literal text "Mandril " via Item::bark, frees the temporary string, and then suspends. This implements the item's short description/response to being looked at.

U8P_BUG::BUG::gotHit: When the object receives a hit it examines incoming parameters and an instance field. If a threshold check (against 0x0100) passes and a referenced NPC/pid reports as dead (Npc::isDead), it iterates a collection of items/elements (driven by the saved first parameter). For each iterated item it queries weight (Item::getWeightIncludingContents) and checks a per-item flag; if weight >= 3 or the flag is set it plays SFX 0x55 and calls Item::receiveHit with fixed small-constant arguments on the item — effectively propagating additional damage/effects to qualifying nearby/heavy items.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven entry for U8P_BUG::look: U8P_BUG::BUG::look runs under Event 0 (examine) and ends with suspend.
Guarded early exits in U8P_BUG::BUG::gotHit: multiple tests return early if the instance field check or Npc::isDead check fails.
Iterative loop in U8P_BUG::BUG::gotHit: the saved first parameter ([BP-02h]) is used to iterate elements; each element can trigger SFX+receiveHit.
Branching per element: weight >= 3 OR per-item flag causes the SFX + receiveHit path; otherwise the loop continues without effect.
open_questions: What exactly are the numeric constants pushed to Item::receiveHit (their damage types/amounts/flags) and how do they map to in-game effects?
What is the exact identity and origin of the iterated collection referenced by the saved first parameter ([BP-02h]) — nearby inventory, equipment slots, or an explicit linked list?
What is the semantic meaning of the tested instance field compared against 0x0100 (attacker type, source mask, or other classifier)?
What precise per-item flag is being compared against 1 (which flag bit/meaning triggers the effect)?

main_function.name: U8P_BUG::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the item's description/response when examined: set info/exclude state on the instance, call Item::bark with the literal string "Mandril ", then suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the actor examines/looks at the object (examine/observe trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_BUG::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_BUG::look handler that emits the item's short description and suspends.

function_map.name: U8P_BUG::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit handler: validate source/attacker conditions, iterate a collection and apply SFX+receiveHit to qualifying items.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/display a short string or message tied to an item/process (used by U8P_BUG::look to show "Mandril ").

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Return whether a referenced NPC/pid is dead; used as a gating condition in gotHit.

function_map.name: Item::getWeightIncludingContents
function_map.role: Return an item's weight including its contents; used to decide whether to apply the hit effect to that item.

function_map.name: U8P_BUG::playSFX
function_map.role: Play a sound effect (called with id 0x55 before applying receiveHit).

function_map.name: Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: Apply hit/damage/effect to an item/NPC instance; invoked with fixed constants on qualifying items.

external_calls.purpose: Emit the literal description string "Mandril " to the examining actor/process.
Check whether a referenced NPC/pid (derived from the instance) is dead; only proceed with the loop if true.
Retrieve per-item weight (including contents) to decide whether the item qualifies for SFX+receiveHit.
Play sound effect 0x55 immediately before applying receiveHit to a qualifying item.
Apply a hit/damage/effect to qualifying items using several small constant arguments (propagates damage/effect).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BUG::BUG::look: When the object is examined (Event 0) it sets instance info/exclude state, emits the literal text "Mandril " via Item::bark, frees the temporary string, and then suspends. This implements the item's short description/response to being looked at.
